div.p: Note to Balloters: This ballot opens two ImplementationGuide elements that previously had no “escape valve” — global.type is retyped to uri and the ig-3 invariant on definition.page.generation is removed — relaxes global.profile to 1..*, and applies several narrative corrections (jurisdiction deprecation note, conformance-declaration guidance, the FHIR Mapping Language relocation, and editorial fixes).

ul.li: ImplementationGuide.global.type retyped from code to uri so cross-version and out-of-band resource types can be referenced [FHIR-55169]
Cardinality of ImplementationGuide.global.profile relaxed to 1..* so a global type may reference more than one profile [FHIR-45138]

ImplementationGuide.definition.page.generation gained an escape valve: the ig-3 invariant was removed and a documentation-generation fallback added, so an unrecognized generator value no longer fails validation [FHIR-55170]

OperationDefinition.resource widened to a URI to allow operations on logical models (cross-referenced here via the shared cross-version resource-type handling; the authoritative change is on OperationDefinition) [FHIR-51651]
The jurisdiction elements are no longer marked deprecated but retain an explanatory note [FHIR-53766]
Conformance-declaration guidance added to the IG notes, resolving the “abstract” flag request as narrative guidance rather than a new element [FHIR-53684]
References updated to reflect that the FHIR Mapping Language is not advancing to normative and has moved to a separate mapping-language IG (with StructureMap) [FHIR-54473]

div.p: An implementation guide (IG) is a set of rules about how FHIR resources are used (or should be used) to solve a particular problem, with associated documentation to support and clarify the usage. Classically, FHIR implementation guides are published on the web after they are generated using the FHIR Implementation Guide Publisher.

The ImplementationGuide resource is a single resource that defines the content of the IG, along with the important entry pages into the publication, as a single logical package, so that the contents of the IG are computable for purposes of publication, validation, registry look-up, and other uses.

In particular, validators are able to use the ImplementationGuide resource to validate content against the implementation guide as a whole. The significant conformance expectation introduced by the ImplementationGuide resource is the idea of Default Profiles. Implementations may conform to multiple implementation guides at once, but this requires that the implementation guides are compatible.
Implementation Guides contain two different types of resource references:

ul.li: Contents: A set of logical statements which implementations must conform to. These are almost always conformance resources
Examples: Examples that illustrate the intent of the profiles defined in the implementation guide. These can be any kind of resource

div.p: An application's Capability Statement may identify one or more implementation guides that an application conforms to.
